_about: Config for universal_civ5_pipeline.py -- the ART / DDS stage of a Civilization V
Brave New World custom-civ build. This is a REUSABLE TEMPLATE: rename the prefix,
swap the placeholder paths/labels/type names for your civ, then run.
This stage builds the icon atlases, alpha/team icons, unit flags, Dawn of Man image,
map image, and leader scene, then writes IconTextureAtlas XML, a DDS manifest, a QA
report, and per-asset previews. It does NOT generate Civilization.sql / Leader.sql /
Trait.sql / Text.sql / Colors.sql / the .modinfo -- those gameplay tables belong to the
separate SQL/text/.modinfo stage. The naming for that stage is documented under the
_civ_design_reference key below, which this art pipeline ignores.
JSON has no comment syntax, so all explanatory text lives in _-prefixed keys that the
pipeline does not read.
Paths are relative to the directory you run the script from. Put your PNG files at the
listed Input/<PREFIX>/ paths. Filenames must NOT carry trailing numeric suffixes like
_1 or _256 (the pipeline appends size suffixes itself).
Run:
python universal_civ5_pipeline.py --config SPARTAN_LEAGUE_CUSTOM.dds.json --check-only
python universal_civ5_pipeline.py --config SPARTAN_LEAGUE_CUSTOM.dds.json
Atlas layout (fixed in code, not set here):
Normal icon atlas: 4x4 grid (16 cells); sizes 256,128,80,64,45,32 (256 atlas = 1024x1024).
Unit flag atlas:   8x8 grid, 32px cells (256x256).
Leader scene:      1024x768 (civ5_leader_scene_4x3) or 1280x720 (civ5_leader_scene_16x9).
Dawn of Man:       1024x768.  Map: 512x512.
The IconTextureAtlas XML it emits already matches this 4x4 / 16-cell layout.
_dds_format_note: DDS pixel formats are chosen per asset category, fixed in code:
Normal icon atlas (civ/leader/unit/building/promotion) -> DXT5 (one combined atlas,
so the leader ICON shares DXT5; the leader 2D SCENE is separate, see below).
Alpha / team icon                                       -> DXT5, except any tile whose
pixel size is not a multiple of 4 (the 45px tile) auto-falls back to uncompressed
A8R8G8B8, because block compression requires mult-of-4 dimensions. Alpha is preserved.
Dawn of Man screen                                      -> DXT1 (no mipmaps).
Leader 2D scene                                         -> DXT1 (with a generated mip chain).
NEVER export icons/flags/alpha as DXT1: it drops the alpha and you get a solid square.
_sizing_note: The subject is CONTAINED at a per-category scale (subject longest side / cell), never
cover-filled, so opaque/full-bleed art cannot blow up to fill the whole circle:
icon_fit_policy 'contain' (default) and 'auto' both apply these exact scales; 'cover' is
legacy and fills/crops the circle (looks larger than Firaxis stock). To override one
asset only, add "safe_subject_scale": <0.0-1.0> to that asset object.
_source_art_naming: Unique unit icon     -> units[].source           -> HomoioiHoplite_Icon.png / KrypteiaScout_Icon.png
Unique unit flag     -> flags[].source           -> HomoioiHoplite_UnitFlag_black.png / KrypteiaScout_UnitFlag_black.png
Unique building icon -> buildings[].source       -> HelotQuarter_Icon.png / SpartanAgogeAssembly_Icon.png
Dawn of Man image    -> dawn_image               -> DawnOfMan_SpartanLeague.png
mod_data_dir: C:/Users/ExampleUser/Documents/My Games/Sid Meier's Civilization 5/MODS/The Spartan League BNW Custom Civ
_mod_data_dir_note: Only used with --write-mod (to drop the DDS into an existing mod folder and refresh .modinfo MD5s). For a plain run or --check-only it is ignored. Leave/rename freely.
_icon_fit_policy_note: contain (default) scales each subject to the per-category target above and leaves the intended margin; auto behaves identically; cover (legacy) fills the whole circle and crops overflow (larger than Firaxis stock).
_alpha_profile_note: Use civ5_alpha_glyph_black_to_transparent when the alpha source is a white logo on a BLACK background. If your alpha source is already white-on-transparent, remove this key (the default path forces the symbol white and keeps the existing alpha).
_leader_scene_profile_note: civ5_leader_scene_4x3 = 1024x768; civ5_leader_scene_16x9 = 1280x720 for widescreen leader art.

_note: NOT read by the art pipeline. This documents the Spartan League art set so the
separate SQL/text/.modinfo stage can consume the same names. Replacement targets,
trait effects, yields, flavors, and gameplay SQL MUST be verified against the
vanilla BNW database before implementation. Anything unmapped belongs in
_mandatory_start_boilerplate_sql: Every normal Ancient-era major civ needs its OWN rows in these BNW join tables, or the
start breaks. These are required boilerplate (not optional):
Civilization_FreeUnits          -> UNITCLASS_SETTLER, UNITAI_SETTLE, Count 1  (the starting Settler; omit it and the civ spawns with no Settler)
Civilization_FreeBuildingClasses-> BUILDINGCLASS_PALACE                       (first founded city behaves/displays as the capital)
Use INSERT (or INSERT OR REPLACE) with the civ's CivilizationType. Custom civs do NOT
inherit these from the base game just because a Civilizations row exists.

mod.name: Spartan League (Custom)
mod.teaser: Adds the Spartan League under Leonidas.
mod.description: A custom Brave New World civilization: the Spartan League, led by Leonidas, with Spartan-themed units, buildings, and support/promotion art.

civilization.starting_bias_note: Set CivilizationType start bias only if supported by the verified DB schema.

leader._flavor_note: Use only FlavorTypes that exist in the vanilla DB. Values are placeholders 1-10.

trait.description_text: PLACEHOLDER trait effect — verify exact gameplay implementation separately.

candidate_effects.description: Placeholder: infantry/phalanx-oriented combat or discipline bonus.
candidate_effects.mapping_status: VERIFY against vanilla BNW tables; otherwise mark Lua-required or UNIMPLEMENTED_EFFECTS.md.

colors._format_note: Civ V Colors are stored as 0.0-1.0 RGBA floats in the Colors table. Values below are placeholders inferred from Spartan red/bronze art direction.
